demographics.occupation: office-based professional (reports reduced work output lately)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I haven't slept at all in three days. There's a review coming up and I'm terrified they'll take away my support, and my whole body is breaking down from trying to prove I'm sick enough. My heart's racing, my hands won't stop shaking, and I can't think straight.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Three days with no sleep before a big disability-support review, and now I feel like I'm physically collapsing and can't cope.

symptom_narrative.onset: over the last 72 hours, building up as an accommodation/support review got closer
symptom_narrative.duration: 3 days of no sleep at all; the underlying body problems are long-standing but feel dramatically worse right now
symptom_narrative.character: wired and exhausted at the same time — heart pounding, hands trembling, mind scattered, and my usual physical problems feel much worse
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: worst I've ever felt, like my body is completely breaking down
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: everywhere — racing heart, shaking hands, and my legs/joints that are normally bad are worse
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant for the last three days, no let-up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: thinking about the review and all the paperwork — the harder I try to prove I'm disabled enough, the worse I feel
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no sleep at all, can't concentrate, feeling on edge and snappy/defensive, drinking a lot of coffee and energy drinks to stay awake and get the forms done
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I get like this every time there's an institutional review, but never this bad
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't work properly and I'm struggling to move around today — I feel like I could just collapse

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a long-standing physical disability affecting my legs and mobility
past_medical_history.how_managed: regular medications and I manage it day to day

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: my usual medications for my disability
a lot of coffee and energy drinks to stay awake for the paperwork

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: Spends huge amounts of time navigating the disability-support system and repeatedly justifying basic needs to access offices. Facing an upcoming accommodation review that feels threatening. Describes feeling dehumanised by having to keep proving how sick they are, and has become defensive and combative in dealing with institutions out of necessity. Reports reduced work output because of the time spent securing accommodations, and a past habit of not asking for adjustments until it caused self-imposed physical strain.

objective_data_offered.value: looks dishevelled and very agitated; paced around at first then tired quickly
speech is fast, pressured and defensive
no sleep at all for about 72 hours
drinking excessive coffee and energy drinks over the last few days to stay awake and organise paperwork
table covered in disorganised medical files, forms and highlighted policy documents
